title: Boston Patriots Founded
description: Billy Sullivan was awarded the eighth and final franchise in the newly formed American Football League. The team was named the Patriots after the Revolutionary War colonists.

title: First AFL Season
description: The Boston Patriots played their inaugural season in the American Football League competing against seven other new franchises.

title: AFL East Division Championship
description: The Patriots defeated the Buffalo Bills 26-8 to claim the AFL Eastern Division championship and earn a berth in the AFL Championship Game.

title: AFL Championship Game Loss
description: The Patriots lost to the San Diego Chargers 51-10 in the AFL Championship Game played in San Diego.

title: Fenway Park Home Games Begin
description: The Patriots began sharing iconic Fenway Park with the Boston Red Sox as a home venue.

title: AFL-NFL Merger Complete
description: The American Football League officially merged with the National Football League. The Boston Patriots joined the AFC East division.

title: Team Renamed New England Patriots
description: The franchise officially changed its geographic name from the Boston Patriots to the New England Patriots to reflect its broader regional fan base.

title: Move to Foxborough
description: The Patriots relocated from Boston to Foxborough
column5: Massachusetts
column6: opening their new stadium in suburban New England.

title: First AFC East Division Title
description: The Patriots won their first AFC East division title under head coach Chuck Fairbanks
column5: posting an 11-5 record.

title: Raymond Berry Hired as Head Coach
description: Raymond Berry
column5: a former Hall of Fame wide receiver and Baltimore Colts assistant
column6: was hired as the Patriots head coach.

title: Super Bowl XX Appearance
description: The Patriots reached Super Bowl XX in New Orleans after posting an 11-5 regular season record and winning three road playoff games.

title: Super Bowl XX Loss to Bears
description: The Patriots were defeated by the Chicago Bears 46-10 in Super Bowl XX in one of the most lopsided Super Bowl losses in history.

title: Victor Kiam Purchases Team
description: Victor Kiam
column5: the Remington Products CEO
column6: purchased the New England Patriots from the Sullivan family.

title: Robert Kraft Buys Foxboro Stadium
description: Robert Kraft purchased Foxboro Stadium out of bankruptcy proceedings
column5: a strategic move that would later give him leverage to acquire the team.

title: 1-15 Season — Franchise Low
description: The Patriots finished with a 1-15 record under head coach Rod Rust
column5: the worst season in the modern history of the franchise.

title: James Orthwein Becomes Owner
description: James Orthwein purchased the New England Patriots from Victor Kiam. Orthwein briefly considered relocating the team to St. Louis.

title: Bill Parcells Hired as Head Coach
description: Bill Parcells
column5: who had won two Super Bowls with the New York Giants
column6: was hired as head coach to rebuild the struggling franchise.

title: Flying Elvis Logo Unveiled
description: The Patriots unveiled a dramatically redesigned logo featuring a patriot in a hiking helmet — immediately dubbed "Flying Elvis" by fans — along with new silver and blue uniforms.

title: Robert Kraft Purchases Team
description: Robert Kraft purchased the New England Patriots for $175 million
column5: keeping the team in New England and beginning the most successful era in franchise history.

title: Super Bowl XXXI Appearance
description: The Patriots reached Super Bowl XXXI in New Orleans under head coach Bill Parcells after finishing 11-5 and winning two playoff games.

title: Super Bowl XXXI Loss to Packers
description: The Patriots lost to the Green Bay Packers 35-21 in Super Bowl XXXI. It was Bill Parcells' final game as head coach.

title: Bill Parcells Departs
description: Bill Parcells resigned as head coach after Super Bowl XXXI despite having two years remaining on his contract
column5: in a bitter split with owner Robert Kraft.

title: Pete Carroll Hired as Head Coach
description: Pete Carroll was hired to replace Bill Parcells as head coach. Carroll led the team to back-to-back playoff appearances in 1997 and 1998.

title: Pete Carroll Dismissed
description: Pete Carroll was dismissed after the Patriots finished 8-8 and missed the playoffs. Carroll would later go on to great success at USC and with the Seattle Seahawks.

title: Bill Belichick Hired as Head Coach
description: Bill Belichick was hired as the 14th head coach in franchise history after a contentious departure from the New York Jets. It began the most successful coach-franchise partnership in NFL history.

title: Tom Brady Drafted in Sixth Round
description: Tom Brady was selected with the 199th pick in the sixth round of the 2000 NFL Draft. The Michigan quarterback would become the greatest quarterback in NFL history.

title: Drew Bledsoe Injured
description: Starting quarterback Drew Bledsoe suffered a serious chest injury after a hit by Jets linebacker Mo Lewis
column5: opening the door for backup Tom Brady.

title: Tom Brady Named Starting Quarterback
description: Bill Belichick named Tom Brady the starting quarterback for the New England Patriots
column5: beginning one of the greatest careers in NFL history.

title: Super Bowl XXXVI Victory
description: The Patriots stunned the heavily favored St. Louis Rams 20-17 in Super Bowl XXXVI on a last-second field goal by Adam Vinatieri. Tom Brady was named Super Bowl MVP.

title: Gillette Stadium Opens
description: Gillette Stadium
column5: a new $400 million facility in Foxborough
column6: opened as the home of the New England Patriots

title: Super Bowl XXXVIII Victory
description: The Patriots defeated the Carolina Panthers 32-29 in a thrilling Super Bowl XXXVIII. Tom Brady won his second Super Bowl MVP award.

title: Super Bowl XXXIX Victory
description: The Patriots defeated the Philadelphia Eagles 24-21 in Super Bowl XXXIX
column5: becoming the first team since the 1993 Cowboys to win back-to-back Super Bowls.

title: Randy Moss Acquired via Trade
description: The Patriots acquired All-Pro wide receiver Randy Moss from the Oakland Raiders for a fourth-round pick
column5: a blockbuster move that transformed the offense.

title: Spygate Scandal
description: The Patriots were found to have illegally videotaped New York Jets coaching signals during a Week 1 game. Bill Belichick was fined $500
column5: 000 and the team forfeited a first-round draft pick.

title: Perfect 16-0 Regular Season
description: The Patriots became the first team in NFL history to complete a perfect 16-game regular season
column5: setting records for points scored and touchdowns. Tom Brady threw 50 touchdown passes and Randy Moss caught 23.

title: Super Bowl XLII Loss to Giants
description: In one of the greatest upsets in Super Bowl history
column5: the New York Giants defeated the Patriots 17-14
column6: ending New England's bid for a perfect 19-0 season. David Tyree's helmet catch became iconic.

title: Tom Brady ACL Injury
description: Tom Brady tore his anterior cruciate ligament in the first series of the season opener against the Kansas City Chiefs. Matt Cassel stepped in and led the team to an 11-5 record.

title: Randy Moss Traded to Vikings
description: The Patriots traded Randy Moss to the Minnesota Vikings midseason after reported friction between Moss and the coaching staff.

title: Super Bowl XLVI Loss to Giants
description: The Patriots lost to the New York Giants 21-17 in Super Bowl XLVI in Indianapolis in a rematch of Super Bowl XLII. Rob Gronkowski played on a high-ankle sprain.

title: Deflategate Scandal Begins
description: After the AFC Championship Game
column5: it was reported that 11 of 12 Patriots footballs were under-inflated. The resulting controversy — "Deflategate" — would result in Tom Brady's four-game suspension.

title: Super Bowl XLIX Victory
description: The Patriots defeated the Seattle Seahawks 28-24 in Super Bowl XLIX in one of the greatest finishes in Super Bowl history. Malcolm Butler's goal-line interception sealed the victory.

title: Tom Brady Suspension — Deflategate
description: Tom Brady served a four-game suspension stemming from the Deflategate investigation after the NFL and courts upheld the penalty. Jimmy Garoppolo started in Brady's absence.

title: Super Bowl LI Victory — Historic Comeback
description: The Patriots overcame a 28-3 deficit — the largest comeback in Super Bowl history — to defeat the Atlanta Falcons 34-28 in overtime in Super Bowl LI. Tom Brady was named MVP.

title: Super Bowl LII Loss to Eagles
description: The Philadelphia Eagles defeated the Patriots 41-33 in Super Bowl LII in Minneapolis. Nick Foles won MVP and the Philly Special trick play became legendary.

title: Super Bowl LIII Victory
description: The Patriots defeated the Los Angeles Rams 13-3 in Super Bowl LIII in Atlanta. It was Tom Brady's sixth championship and Bill Belichick's sixth as head coach
column5: both NFL records.

title: Tom Brady Departs in Free Agency
description: Tom Brady officially left the New England Patriots after 20 seasons
column5: six Super Bowl championships
column6: and four MVP awards to sign with the Tampa Bay Buccaneers.

title: Patriots Miss Playoffs
description: The Patriots finished 7-9 and missed the playoffs for the first time since 2008
column5: ending their streak of 11 consecutive AFC East division titles.

title: Bill Belichick Surpasses Don Shula
description: Bill Belichick recorded his 325th career regular season win
column5: surpassing Don Shula's all-time record to become the winningest coach in NFL history.

title: Bill Belichick Departs After 24 Seasons
description: The Patriots and Bill Belichick mutually agreed to part ways after 24 seasons together. Belichick finished with a 333-165 regular season record and six Super Bowl championships.

title: Drake Maye Selected 3rd Overall
description: The Patriots selected quarterback Drake Maye with the 3rd overall pick in the 2024 NFL Draft
column5: selecting their franchise quarterback of the future out of the University of North Carolina.

title: Jerod Mayo Named Head Coach
description: Jerod Mayo
column5: a former Patriots linebacker and team captain
column6: was named the 16th head coach in franchise history at just 38 years old.

title: Mike Vrabel Hired as Head Coach
description: Mike Vrabel
column5: a former Patriots linebacker and Super Bowl champion
column6: was hired as head coach after Jerod Mayo was dismissed following a 4-13 season.
